workflow_name: QBR Transcript -> Health + Whitespace + Renewal Risk
user_message: Just got this from Karen Berg at Iron Creek Mining -- I need help figuring out whitespace analysis. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: whitespace analysis Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-02-17
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on whitespace analysis from our last session.

[00:05:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:07:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:09:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:24:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Iron Creek Mining: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF45-M trap (Rigid QBR-template output doesn't accommodate atypical QBRs (technical-led, exec); produces complete Health delta assessment, whitespace hypotheses, renewal risk with confidence, action distribution wi
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF45-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF45-M failure mode (Rigid QBR-template output doesn't accommodate atypical QBRs (technical-led, exec)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF45-M -- Rigid QBR-template output doesn't accommodate atypical QBRs (technical-led, exec
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
